description: Left-origin lateral truck crossing on a normal two-lane road where the nominal rightward escape path is occupied by a pedestrian; a close follower makes pure hard braking contested. The truck is instantiated on a collision course for each TTC/lateral-speed pair, with lateral distance determined by construction.
hypothesis: When the right escape side is occupied by a pedestrian, the law should switch from rightward threat-drift to braking/hold-lane at moderate TTC, but may only accept a constrained right drift at the shortest TTC when follower risk and braking insufficiency dominate.
